_comment: Codex connection registry. Layer 4 binding artifact. Every cross-unit / cross-strand connection is registered here with a stable ID, anchor phrase, synthesis note, and the units/flows that invoke it. Read by every agent before producing content; updated by the integration agent after each batch.

connections.anchor_phrase: canonical sheaf dual to Serre duality
connections.synthesis_note: $\omega_X$ is the dualising sheaf appearing in the duality.

connections.anchor_phrase: Riemann-Roch (curves) equivalent to Riemann-Roch (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Riemann-Roch for compact Riemann surfaces — the analytic version, equivalent via Serre's GAGA.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging scheme and Riemann-Roch (curves)
connections.synthesis_note: The algebraic-geometric setting; smooth projective curves are 1-dimensional smooth proper schemes.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Serre duality and Hodge decomposition

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Serre duality and Kodaira vanishing
connections.synthesis_note: Serre duality dualises Kodaira vanishing into the Akizuki-Nakano statement.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging sheaf cohomology and Riemann-Roch (curves)
connections.synthesis_note: Direct application of sheaf cohomology of line bundles on a curve, computing $\dim H^0(C; \mathcal{L}) - \dim H^1(C; \mathcal{L})$.

connections.anchor_phrase: sheaf cohomology equivalent to Riemann-Roch (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Riemann-Roch for compact Riemann surfaces — analytic version, equivalent to the algebraic statement via GAGA.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Chern-Weil and Atiyah-Singer index

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Pontryagin/Chern and Atiyah-Singer index
connections.synthesis_note: Characteristic classes are the topological side of the formula.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Pontryagin/Chern and Bott periodicity
connections.synthesis_note: Chern classes and Chern characters mediate between vector bundles and cohomology in complex $K$-theory.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging holomorphic function and Riemann-Roch (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Riemann-Roch for compact Riemann surfaces — the dimension formula for spaces of holomorphic sections of line bundles, the analytic version of algebraic Riemann-Roch.

connections.anchor_phrase: Riemann surface equivalent to Riemann-Roch (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Riemann-Roch for compact Riemann surfaces — the dimension formula on compact Riemann surfaces, equivalent to algebraic Riemann-Roch.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging exterior derivative and Stokes
connections.synthesis_note: Relates $d$ to integration via $\int_M d\omega = \int_{\partial M} \omega$.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Yang-Mills and Atiyah-Singer index
connections.synthesis_note: Yang-Mills action — elliptic deformation complexes in gauge theory use index formulas to compute expected moduli dimensions.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Stable homotopy and Homotopy and homotopy group
connections.synthesis_note: Stable homotopy depends on homotopy theory, suspension, and spheres. It connects to classifying spaces through stable classical groups and to topological K-theory through spectra and Bott periodicity.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging character and Frobenius reciprocity
connections.synthesis_note: The character form $\langle \chi_{\mathrm{Ind}}, \chi_V \rangle_G = \langle \chi_W, \chi_V|_H \rangle_H$ reduces multiplicity computation to subgroup arithmetic.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging character and Maschke
connections.synthesis_note: Characters are well-defined invariants because of complete reducibility — every representation has a character determined by its irreducible decomposition.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Character orthogonality and Maschke
connections.synthesis_note: Complete reducibility ensures every representation has a unique decomposition into orthogonal isotypic components.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging group rep and Schur
connections.synthesis_note: Schur's lemma is the foundational result organising the structure of representations.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging regular rep and Frobenius reciprocity
connections.synthesis_note: The regular-representation decomposition is a direct application of Frobenius reciprocity to $\mathrm{Ind}_{\{e\}}^G \mathbb{C}$.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging regular rep and Maschke
connections.synthesis_note: The regular representation $\mathbb{C}G$ decomposes via Maschke into irreducible isotypic components, giving the dimension formula $\sum (\dim V)^2 = |G|$.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Specht module and Young diagram
connections.synthesis_note: (symmetric group representation) is the parent unit; Young diagrams are its combinatorial backbone, and each irreducible $V^\lambda$ is built from a tableau of shape $\lambda$. (Specht module) gives the modular construction of $V^\lambda$ via polytabloids — explicit basis vectors built from tableaux.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging symmetric group rep and Specht module
connections.synthesis_note: (symmetric group representation) is the parent unit; Specht modules are the *characteristic-flexible* realisation of the irreducibles classified by Frobenius-Young. (Young diagrams) provides the combinatorial scaffolding — every Specht module has an explicit basis indexed by standard Young tableaux.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging symmetric group rep and Young diagram

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Clifford algebra and Bott periodicity
connections.synthesis_note: The eight-fold real / two-fold complex Clifford periodicities ($\mathrm{Cl}_{p, q+8} \cong \mathrm{Cl}_{p,q} \otimes M_{16}(\mathbb{R})$ and $\mathrm{Cl}_{2k} \cong M_{2^k}(\mathbb{C})$) are not coincidences but the algebraic shadow of Bott periodicity in $KO$- and $K$-theory.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Blowup and Riemann-Roch (curves)

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging coherent sheaf and Riemann-Roch (curves)

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Kodaira vanishing and Riemann-Roch (curves)

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Kodaira vanishing and sheaf cohomology

connections.anchor_phrase: Riemann surface equivalent to scheme

connections.synthesis_note: Computes the index of the chiral Dirac operator using spinor-bundle data.

connections.synthesis_note: The Cartan-Serre-Grothendieck criterion is a coherent-sheaf property.

connections.synthesis_note: $\mathcal{O}(d)$ on $\mathbb{P}^n$ is the canonical example of an ample (very ample for $d \geq 1$) bundle.

connections.anchor_phrase: Hodge decomposition built on canonical sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: $\omega_X = \Omega^n$ is the top component, contributing $H^{n, q}$ to the Hodge decomposition.

connections.anchor_phrase: Kodaira vanishing built on canonical sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: Vanishing $H^i(X; \omega_X \otimes L) = 0$ for $L$ ample, $i > 0$.

connections.anchor_phrase: Moduli of curves built on canonical sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: The canonical line bundle on $\overline{\mathcal{M}_g}$ relates to tautological classes $\kappa, \lambda$.

connections.anchor_phrase: morphism (schemes) built on Direct and inverse image of sheaves

connections.anchor_phrase: Kodaira vanishing built on Hodge decomposition

connections.anchor_phrase: ample line bundle built on line bundle (scheme)

connections.anchor_phrase: Cartier divisor built on line bundle (scheme)
connections.synthesis_note: Cartier divisors and line bundles correspond bijectively.

connections.anchor_phrase: coherent sheaf built on line bundle (scheme)

connections.anchor_phrase: quasi-coherent sheaf built on line bundle (scheme)
connections.synthesis_note: Line bundles are rank-1 locally free quasi-coherent sheaves.

connections.anchor_phrase: Geometric invariant theory built on Moduli of curves
connections.synthesis_note: Mumford's original GIT application; constructs $\mathcal{M}_g$ as a quasi-projective scheme.

connections.anchor_phrase: ample line bundle built on Picard group
connections.synthesis_note: The ample cone is the natural positivity structure inside $\mathrm{Pic}(X) \otimes \mathbb{R}$.

connections.anchor_phrase: Cartier divisor built on Picard group
connections.synthesis_note: Defined as Cartier divisors modulo principal divisors (or equivalently as line bundle isomorphism classes).

connections.anchor_phrase: line bundle (scheme) built on Picard group
connections.synthesis_note: $\mathrm{Pic}(X)$ is by definition the abelian group of line bundles up to isomorphism.

connections.anchor_phrase: projective space built on Picard group
connections.synthesis_note: $\mathrm{Pic}(\mathbb{P}^n_k) = \mathbb{Z}$ is the canonical foundational example.

connections.anchor_phrase: projective space built on projective scheme
connections.synthesis_note: $\mathbb{P}^n_k = \mathrm{Proj}\,k[x_0, \ldots, x_n]$ is the universal example.

connections.anchor_phrase: quasi-coherent sheaf built on projective scheme
connections.synthesis_note: Quasi-coherent sheaf and Coherent sheaf — coherent sheaves on $\mathrm{Proj}(S)$ correspond to graded $S$-modules modulo torsion.

connections.anchor_phrase: coherent sheaf built on quasi-coherent sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: Coherent sheaves are quasi-coherent + finiteness conditions.

connections.anchor_phrase: scheme built on Sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: Schemes are locally ringed spaces; their structure sheaf is a sheaf of rings.

connections.anchor_phrase: Riemann surface built on Sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: Sheaves of holomorphic functions, meromorphic functions, and differentials on a Riemann surface.

connections.anchor_phrase: canonical sheaf built on sheaf of differentials
connections.synthesis_note: $\omega_X = \det \Omega^1_X$, the top exterior power.

connections.anchor_phrase: Hodge decomposition built on sheaf of differentials
connections.synthesis_note: Relies on the spectral sequence $H^q(\Omega^p) \Rightarrow H^{p+q}_{\mathrm{dR}}$.

connections.anchor_phrase: Direct and inverse image of sheaves built on sheafification
connections.synthesis_note: The inverse-image presheaf is generally not a sheaf; $f^{-1}\mathcal{G}$ is its sheafification.

connections.anchor_phrase: direct/inverse image built on stalk
connections.synthesis_note: The inverse image $f^{-1}\mathcal{G}$ at a point $x$ has stalk $\mathcal{G}_{f(x)}$; this is the key stalk-functoriality property.

connections.anchor_phrase: sheafification built on Stalk of a sheaf
connections.synthesis_note: Sheafification preserves stalks: $\mathcal{F}_x = (\mathcal{F}^+)_x$ for any presheaf $\mathcal{F}$.

connections.anchor_phrase: Cartier divisor built on Weil divisor
connections.synthesis_note: Weil and Cartier divisors agree on locally factorial schemes; differ in general.

connections.anchor_phrase: line bundle (scheme) built on Weil divisor
connections.synthesis_note: Weil divisors and line bundles agree via $\mathrm{Pic} = \mathrm{Cl}$ on locally factorial schemes.

connections.anchor_phrase: Picard group built on Weil divisor
connections.synthesis_note: Divisor class group of locally factorial schemes equals the Picard group; in general there is an injection $\mathrm{Pic}(X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Cl}(X)$ for normal $X$.

connections.anchor_phrase: Ideal in an algebra built on associative algebra
connections.synthesis_note: Associative algebra supplies the multiplication that ideals must absorb.

connections.anchor_phrase: group action built on group
connections.synthesis_note: Group actions build on groups and feed principal bundles, where each fiber is a right torsor for the structure group. Lie groups act smoothly on manifolds, producing homogeneous spaces and associated bundles.

connections.anchor_phrase: orthogonal group built on group
connections.synthesis_note: Orthogonal groups depend on groups, bilinear forms, and Lie groups. They act on orthonormal frames, giving the structure group of the orthogonal frame bundle.

connections.anchor_phrase: Quotient algebra built on tensor algebra
connections.synthesis_note: Quotient algebra follows tensor algebra in the Clifford chain. Clifford algebra is the quotient of a tensor algebra by the quadratic-form ideal from.

connections.anchor_phrase: tensor algebra built on tensor product
connections.synthesis_note: Tensor algebra depends on vector spaces and the tensor product of vector spaces. It feeds directly into quotient algebra, where relations are imposed by two-sided ideals.

connections.anchor_phrase: Chern-Weil built on complex vector bundle
connections.synthesis_note: Chern classes have de Rham representatives via curvature of a Hermitian connection.

connections.anchor_phrase: vector bundle built on principal bundle
connections.synthesis_note: Frame bundles are principal bundles, and vector bundles are associated bundles.

connections.anchor_phrase: Chern-Weil built on principal bundle
connections.synthesis_note: Chern-Weil theory is a construction on principal bundles before it becomes a theory of vector bundles.

connections.anchor_phrase: Principal bundle with connection built on principal bundle
connections.synthesis_note: A connection is additional differential data on a principal bundle.

connections.anchor_phrase: Yang-Mills built on Chern-Weil
connections.synthesis_note: Gauge theory uses the same curvature forms, though with metric-dependent action functionals rather than topological cohomology classes.

connections.anchor_phrase: Chern-Weil built on Pontryagin/Chern
connections.synthesis_note: Supplies curvature representatives for the real de Rham images of Chern and Pontryagin classes.

connections.anchor_phrase: Dirac operator built on Pontryagin/Chern
connections.synthesis_note: The Â-class and Chern character in the index formula are built from these.

connections.anchor_phrase: Pontryagin/Chern built on Stiefel-Whitney
connections.synthesis_note: Stiefel-Whitney classes — mod-2 companions for real bundles; $w_2$ obstructs spin structures.

connections.anchor_phrase: spin structure built on Stiefel-Whitney
connections.synthesis_note: Stiefel-Whitney classes — the precise cohomological obstruction $w_2$ to spin existence; relatedly $w_1$ for orientability and $w_1^2 + w_2$ for Pin⁻.

connections.anchor_phrase: holomorphic line bundle (RS) built on divisor (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Supplies the local analytic language, supplies the Riemann-surface setting, and uses this unit as part of the global theory of curves, periods, or sheaf cohomology. The same ideas also interact with divisor and line-bundle constructions in and.

connections.anchor_phrase: Jacobian variety built on divisor (RS)
connections.synthesis_note: Supplies the local analytic language, supplies the Riemann-surface setting, and uses this unit as part of the global theory of curves, periods, or sheaf cohomology. The same ideas also interact with divisor and line-bundle constructions in and.

connections.anchor_phrase: Jacobian variety built on holomorphic line bundle (RS)

connections.anchor_phrase: Chern-Weil built on Principal bundle with connection

connections.anchor_phrase: Principal bundle with connection built on connection (vector bundle)

connections.anchor_phrase: Variational calculus on manifolds built on integration on manifolds

connections.anchor_phrase: elliptic operators built on symbol

connections.anchor_phrase: compact operators built on Banach space

connections.anchor_phrase: Fredholm operators built on Banach space

connections.anchor_phrase: Hilbert space built on Banach space
connections.synthesis_note: Hilbert spaces are Banach spaces whose norm comes from an inner product.

connections.anchor_phrase: Banach space built on bounded operators
connections.synthesis_note: Operator norms and continuity are formulated between Banach spaces.

connections.anchor_phrase: compact operators built on bounded operators
connections.synthesis_note: $\mathcal{K}(X) \subseteq \mathcal{B}(X)$ as a closed two-sided ideal.

connections.anchor_phrase: Fredholm operators built on bounded operators
connections.synthesis_note: Bounded operators invertible modulo compacts; the Atkinson characterisation.

connections.anchor_phrase: Fredholm operators built on compact operators
connections.synthesis_note: Fredholm $\iff$ invertible modulo $\mathcal{K}$ in $\mathcal{B}(X) / \mathcal{K}(X)$ (Atkinson).

connections.anchor_phrase: elliptic operators built on Fredholm operators

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Fredholm operators and Atiyah-Singer index
connections.synthesis_note: The topological formula for the Fredholm index.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Fredholm operators and Bott periodicity

connections.anchor_phrase: Dirac operator built on Fredholm operators

connections.anchor_phrase: inner product space built on normed space
connections.synthesis_note: Inner product spaces depend on normed vector spaces and bilinear forms. Hilbert spaces are complete inner product spaces.

connections.anchor_phrase: CFT basics built on Yang-Mills
connections.synthesis_note: Two-dimensional gauge and conformal theories share stress-energy and symmetry methods, though Yang-Mills itself is not conformal in every dimension.

connections.anchor_phrase: infinite-dim reps built on central extension
connections.synthesis_note: This unit depends on Lie algebras, Hilbert-space language, and central extensions. It feeds Virasoro algebra, where highest-weight and positive-energy representations are primary examples.

connections.anchor_phrase: Virasoro generalises central extension

connections.anchor_phrase: Virasoro built on infinite-dim reps

connections.synthesis_note: The same two-valued monodromy viewpoint appears in characteristic-class obstructions: the failure to lift structure groups is recorded by cohomological data, especially Stiefel-Whitney classes in the spin case.

connections.anchor_phrase: spin group built on double cover
connections.synthesis_note: Double covers connect covering-space theory to principal bundles. The map $\mathrm{Spin}(n)\to\mathrm{SO}(n)$ is a double cover used by spin group theory.

connections.anchor_phrase: spin structure built on double cover
connections.synthesis_note: Double covers connect covering-space theory to principal bundles. The map $\mathrm{Spin}(n)\to\mathrm{SO}(n)$ is a double cover used by spin group theory.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Homotopy and homotopy group and Bott periodicity
connections.synthesis_note: Periodicity of stable homotopy of classical groups.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging Spectrum and Bott periodicity
connections.synthesis_note: The structure maps of $\mathbf{KU}$ via Bott periodicity.

connections.anchor_phrase: Clifford chessboard equivalent to real Bott periodicity
connections.synthesis_note: Atiyah-Bott-Shapiro identify the module quotient \widehat{\mathfrak{M}}_n \cong KO^{-n}(\mathrm{pt}), exposing the eight-fold algebraic rhythm of \mathrm{Cl}_{p,q} as the algebraic shadow of real Bott periodicity in KO-theory.

connections.anchor_phrase: KR (1,1)-periodicity equivalent to Cl_{r+1,s+1} = Cl_{r,s} ⊗ Cl_{1,1}
connections.synthesis_note: The K-theoretic statement and the algebraic statement are the same theorem at different abstraction levels; both rest on \mathrm{Cl}_{1,1} \cong M_2(\mathbb{R}) and a contractibility argument for \mathrm{Cl}_{1,1}-module structures.

connections.anchor_phrase: calibrations built on Spin(7)/G_2 spinor squaring via triality
connections.synthesis_note: The Spin(7) and G_2 calibrating forms of Harvey-Lawson 1982 are constructed by spinor squaring under triality; calibrated submanifolds are exactly those preserved by this form's pointwise structure. Forward-promise: target unit ships in Batch 2.

connections.anchor_phrase: Hodge Laplacian as Dirac square of the de Rham Dirac bundle
connections.synthesis_note: The Hodge Laplacian on differential forms is the Dirac square of the de Rham Dirac bundle (\Lambda^* T^*M, c(\xi) = \xi\wedge - \iota_{\xi^\sharp}, \nabla^{LC}); the Bochner curvature operator is the obstruction tensor for harmonic forms.

connections.anchor_phrase: α-invariant built on Cl_k-linear Dirac index in KO
connections.synthesis_note: Hitchin's α-invariant on a closed spin manifold is the Clifford-index of the spin Dirac operator viewed as a Cl_n-linear Dirac bundle; the entire psc obstruction chain (Hitchin → Gromov-Lawson → Stolz) is built on this Cl_k-linear refinement. Forward-promise: target unit ships in Batch 2 as 03.09.16.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging McKean-Singer supertrace and analytic index
connections.synthesis_note: For every t > 0, Str(e^{-tD^2}) = ind(D^+); the eigenspace pairing under D collapses every positive-eigenvalue contribution. The heat-kernel proof of Atiyah-Singer takes this as its starting point and evaluates the supertrace at small t.

connections.anchor_phrase: Fredholmness of elliptic operators built on parametrix construction
connections.synthesis_note: An elliptic pseudodifferential operator A admits a parametrix Q in Ψ^{-m} with QA-I, AQ-I smoothing. Smoothing operators are compact between Sobolev spaces; Atkinson identifies operators invertible modulo compacts as Fredholm. The parametrix construction is therefore the analytic substrate of every elliptic-Fredholm theorem.

connections.anchor_phrase: family-index Chern character lives in cohomology of the base
connections.synthesis_note: For a smooth family of elliptic operators D = {D_b} over B, ind(D) ∈ K^0(B) and ch(ind(D)) = π_*(ch(σ) Td(T_{M/B} ⊗ ℂ)) ∈ H^*(B; ℚ) by the Atiyah-Hirzebruch Riemann-Roch identity. The Bismut superconnection produces the cohomology representative as a closed differential form.

connections.anchor_phrase: equivariant index localises to the fixed-point set
connections.synthesis_note: For a G-equivariant elliptic operator D with isolated non-degenerate fixed points of g ∈ G, the equivariant character of ind_G(D) at g equals a sum of local contributions at M^g built from \hat{A}, ch, and det(I - dg^{-1}|_{T_pM}). Atiyah-Bott 1968 fixed-point formula is recovered by the equivariant heat-kernel localisation argument.

connections.anchor_phrase: psc obstruction chain built on Lichnerowicz formula
connections.synthesis_note: The spinor-Bochner identity D^2 = nabla^* nabla + (1/4) Scal is the structural foundation of the entire psc obstruction chain. Without Lichnerowicz, no Hitchin-α; without Hitchin, no Gromov-Lawson; without Gromov-Lawson, no Stolz classification.

connections.anchor_phrase: α-invariant equivalent to Cl_n-linear Clifford-index of the spin Dirac operator
connections.synthesis_note: Hitchin's α-invariant on a closed spin manifold is exactly the Cl_n-linear Clifford-index of the spin Dirac operator viewed as a Cl_n-linear Dirac bundle. Equivalence of two definitions: one analytical (kernel of D as graded Cl_n-module), one K-theoretic (image under Atiyah-Bott-Shapiro orientation).

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging enlargeable topology and Lichnerowicz vanishing
connections.synthesis_note: Gromov-Lawson 1983 §V: a closed enlargeable spin manifold admits no metric of positive scalar curvature. The bridge converts a topological condition (small-Lipschitz maps to S^n from finite covers) into an analytic Lichnerowicz vanishing via twisted Dirac operators.

connections.anchor_phrase: theorem bridging asymptotic flatness and harmonic spinor identity
connections.synthesis_note: Witten 1981 bridges the ADM-mass formalism (general relativity, asymptotic geometry) and the Dirac-spinor formalism (analytic differential geometry) via a single integration-by-parts identity. The boundary term of Lichnerowicz on an AF spin 3-manifold equals -4π m_ADM.

connections.anchor_phrase: Berger holonomy bijection with parallel spinors
connections.synthesis_note: Wang 1989: a simply-connected Riemannian spin manifold admits a non-zero parallel spinor iff its restricted holonomy is one of SU(n), Sp(n), G_2, Spin(7). Parallel-spinor counts: 2, n+1, 1, 1 respectively. The bijection is the structural foundation of calibrated geometry.

connections.anchor_phrase: calibrations require special holonomy structure
connections.synthesis_note: Each Harvey-Lawson calibrated geometry lives on a manifold with one of Wang's special holonomy groups — SU(n) for SL, Sp(n) for hyperkähler, G_2 for associative/coassociative, Spin(7) for Cayley. The calibrating form is the spinor square of the parallel spinor.

connections.anchor_phrase: Mayer-Vietoris sequence is the foundation for good-cover induction
connections.synthesis_note: The MV exact sequence is the engine that good-cover induction iterates to produce finite-dimensionality of de Rham cohomology on every compact manifold.

connections.anchor_phrase: de Rham theorem built on Mayer-Vietoris induction over a good cover
connections.synthesis_note: The de Rham theorem with R-coefficients is proved by comparing the de Rham and singular MV sequences on a good cover, with the integration pairing as the comparison map.

connections.anchor_phrase: Čech-de Rham double complex built on a good cover
connections.synthesis_note: The good-cover hypothesis makes column exactness in the double complex hold; without it, the column collapse fails and the double complex carries spectral-sequence content rather than collapse content.

connections.anchor_phrase: tic-tac-toe Künneth equivalent to MV-induction Künneth on finite-good-cover manifolds
connections.synthesis_note: Künneth admits two proofs: MV induction (Bott-Tu §5) and tic-tac-toe ascent on the product Čech-de Rham double complex (§9). Bott-Tu's dual-proof discipline.

connections.anchor_phrase: de Rham theorem built on Čech-de Rham double-complex collapse
connections.synthesis_note: Weil's 1952 proof: row-collapse to H*_dR; column-collapse to Čech of the constant sheaf R = simplicial cohomology of the nerve = singular cohomology with R-coefficients.

connections.anchor_phrase: de Rham Thom class equivalent to Chern-Weil Euler form
connections.synthesis_note: For oriented Euclidean rank-r bundle E with metric connection, e(E) admits two de Rham realisations: i*Φ (zero-section pullback of Thom class) and (1/(2π)^{r/2})Pf(Ω) (Chern-Weil); the two routes give the same cohomology class.

connections.anchor_phrase: spin-geometry Â-genus machinery built on the global angular form
connections.synthesis_note: On a spin manifold, ψ on the spinor unit-sphere bundle with sign dψ = -π* e(E) is structural input for Â-genus density in local Atiyah-Singer; both Bismut superconnection and Getzler rescaling use this convention.

connections.anchor_phrase: Thom isomorphism equivalent to relative de Rham of the disc-sphere pair
connections.synthesis_note: H*_{cv}(E) ≅ H*(D(E), S(E)); the Thom class corresponds to the relative fundamental class. Relative LES + Thom iso = Gysin sequence.

connections.anchor_phrase: Leray-Hirsch built on Künneth on each fiber
connections.synthesis_note: When fibre cohomology is finite-dim and a global lift exists, the Leray-Hirsch theorem reduces to fibrewise Künneth times base cohomology.

connections.anchor_phrase: de Rham cohomology equivalent to singular cohomology with real coefficients (three routes)
connections.synthesis_note: Three independent proofs: MV induction over a good cover, Čech-de Rham column collapse (Weil 1952), sheaf-cohomology fine resolution (Leray).

connections.anchor_phrase: local system on connected X equivalent to π_1(X)-representation
connections.synthesis_note: The category of local systems on a connected pointed space is equivalent to the category of π_1(X)-representations via monodromy; a structural insight from Poincaré 1883.

connections.anchor_phrase: twisted de Rham complex built on orientation local system
connections.synthesis_note: On a non-orientable manifold, twisted Poincaré duality uses the orientation local system; the twisted de Rham complex computes cohomology with this twist.

connections.anchor_phrase: exact-couple spectral sequence equivalent to the Čech-de Rham double-complex spectral sequence
connections.synthesis_note: The two filtrations on the Čech-de Rham double complex give two spectral sequences; both are concrete instances of the abstract exact-couple machinery (Massey 1952). The reader has already done several spectral sequences before meeting the abstract definition.

connections.anchor_phrase: Serre spectral sequence is the filtered-complex SS of a fibration's singular cochain filtration
connections.synthesis_note: Serre 1951 specialises the abstract spectral-sequence machinery to a Serre fibration; the convergence is to the cohomology of the total space, with E_2 = H^p(B; H^q(F)).

connections.anchor_phrase: Gysin sequence + Euler class derived from Serre spectral sequence of an oriented sphere bundle
connections.synthesis_note: For an oriented S^{r-1}-bundle, the Serre SS collapses at E_2 except for one differential of bidegree (r, 1-r); that differential is multiplication by the Euler class, recovering the Gysin LES.

connections.anchor_phrase: Serre SS of path-loop fibration computes loop-space cohomology and π_n(S^k)
connections.synthesis_note: Serre 1951's path-loop fibration ΩX → PX → X with PX contractible gives an SS converging to a point and so allows ΩX cohomology to be read off; π_4(S^3) = Z/2 is computed via this device.

connections.anchor_phrase: splitting principle built on Leray-Hirsch theorem applied iteratively to flag-bundle projections
connections.synthesis_note: Iterating Leray-Hirsch on the flag-bundle Fl(E) → M splits E formally as a sum of line bundles after pulling back to Fl(E); characteristic classes are then computed as elementary symmetric functions of formal Chern roots.

connections.anchor_phrase: splitting principle equivalent to Borel presentation H*(BG) = H*(BT)^W
connections.synthesis_note: Borel-Hirzebruch 1958/59: the universal splitting principle is the cohomology fact H*(BG; Q) = H*(BT; Q)^W; for G = U(n), W = S_n acts by permuting Chern roots.

connections.anchor_phrase: Hopf index theorem built on global angular form and integration of Euler class
connections.synthesis_note: Bott-Tu §11 derivation of Σ ind_p(V) = χ(M) rests on the global angular form ψ with fiber-integral 1 and dψ = -π* e(E), then applies Stokes in de Rham.

connections.anchor_phrase: Sullivan minimal model encodes rational homotopy type for simply-connected finite-type spaces
connections.synthesis_note: Sullivan's main theorem (1977 Publ. IHÉS 47): the indecomposable space V^n of the minimal model is dual to π_n(X) ⊗ ℚ; the differential encodes Whitehead products and higher Massey products. Rational Hurewicz is the bottom-degree statement.

connections.anchor_phrase: Sullivan model built on de Rham complex of polynomial forms
connections.synthesis_note: The piecewise-polynomial functor A_PL is the rational refinement of the smooth de Rham complex; on a smooth manifold A_PL(X) ⊗ ℝ is quasi-isomorphic to Ω*(X).

connections.anchor_phrase: Whitehead tower equivalent to dual Postnikov tower for connectivity
connections.synthesis_note: Postnikov truncates from above; Whitehead tower from below. Each stage is a principal K(π, n)-fibration; the two towers bracket homotopy structure.

connections.anchor_phrase: Finiteness of π_k(S^n) for k > n built on Whitehead tower and Serre SS
connections.synthesis_note: Serre 1953 Annals 58: π_k(S^n) finite for k > n except π_{4j-1}(S^{2j}) which has Z-summand from Hopf invariant. Proof via rational Hurewicz on Whitehead tower + finite-generation.

connections.anchor_phrase: Universal complex rank-k bundle γ_k = colim γ_k^n on infinite Grassmannian, equivalent to BU(k)
connections.synthesis_note: Steenrod 1951; Milnor 1956: G_k(ℂ^∞) carries the universal complex rank-k bundle, equivalent up to homotopy to BU(k). Pullback implements [X, BU(k)] ↔ Vect^k_ℂ(X).

connections.anchor_phrase: Schubert calculus on Grassmannian built on Borel presentation of flag-manifold cohomology
connections.synthesis_note: The Borel presentation H*(Fl_n; ℤ) = ℤ[x_1,…,x_n]/⟨e_1,…,e_n⟩ (S_n coinvariants) realises Schubert classes via BGG-Demazure 1973-74 divided-difference operators.
